transcript.clean_text: stage 12 of the jitter the italia saw big breakaway get away and three of the members of that break contest the finale to the line in the city of genoa for the second day running it be a young italian who won stefan aldani winning his first ever professional bike race alpacine fedex taking their second stage when it was judo and juanpe lopez would start stage 13 in the mayarosa stage 13 took the riders to san remo not the beginning of spring but at the end of it and in summer temperatures a really hot day for a 150 kilometer ride leaving san remo heading over the hills away from liguria and into piemonte where the stage would finish in cornell on paper one that the sprinters should take but at this stage of the race and with tired legs the breakaway was always going to have a chance over the coledy nava the hardest climb of the day and then valley roads all the way up to cornell for a finish on stage 13 the last possible sprint until almost the end of the race it was on san francisco the peloton assembled leaving the port and heading away there were a few attacks for the first 10 minutes or so it wasn't easy to get a break established but after 10 kilometers or so of trying things began to settle down five riders found themselves up the road predom foreign and their early advantage would be controlled at just about three minutes the sprint team setting out their stall obama quick step israel and uae all working there was time to celebrate for stefan aldani after a stage win but behind there was bad news for dsm roman balder looking brilliant for the first 12 days of the judo had apparently been sick overnight and could not continue fourth place on the gc one of the favorites to win the judo out through illness on day 13. i'm lucky for him we got to the intermediate sprints five riders still together and filippo taliani took it increasing his own lead in the intermediate sprint classification for the malaya chiclamino points well the rest of them were taken by the maya chiclamino himself nobody really interested in challenging alma dimar who had a commanding lead three more points to his lead arnold de mar looking really good in charge of the points competition then they headed to the coledi nava climb would be the one that would decide exactly how things would pan out on the day taliani was dropped from the breakaway on the way up and the break actually increased their lead doubled their lead in fact by the time they got to the top incorn was in the breakaway for the second successive day and he was pushing on almost seven minutes with 90 kilometers to go the peloton would have to work for it the riders riding the reverse sambremor pandemic route from august 2020 and heading up to cornell same sprint teams were still committed at this point the gap just below six minutes with 72 k's to go with 30 kilometers left just over three minutes the gap and the breakers began to dream behind they will begin to panic and suddenly everything was in the balance the win was playing too richie port the biggest name in a group that was called out in a split at the back 30 or so riders missing out there inside the final 25 kilometers and still almost three minutes the fans watching dared to dream of a breakaway success had the peloton made a miscalculation 10ks to go became two k's to go 20 seconds still the gap but an uphill section and more teams getting interested behind in the peloton meant that the breakaway stage were numbered an attack from fonnenberg inside the last kilometer meant that that would be that they'd be caught rather quickly maestro would put in one final dig but it would not be enough under the cobbles of cornell town center obama fdg starting the lead out with ramon cinco down he caught with 600 meters to go actually going off to the side and jacob launching the sprint from the center demar hit the front he'd stay in front cavendish tried to come around and got close but not close enough bauhaus was the closest but it would still be arnold imar who put in a hat trick of wins showing that he's the strongest sprinter at this particular gyrod italian terrific teamwork from group you could say the same about quickstep as well they rode all day long as did israel but it was demar who finished the job almost for the breakaway hot break for the attackers delight for the frenchman and arnold de mar winning a third zero stage in 12 days them are then taking the stage ahead of bar house cavendish in third gabiri at dynasty in the top five more celebrations for the man who held on to the malia chiclamino and increased his lead by a lot of points he'll have to concentrate on making the time limit now if he's to take that jersey home to verona juanpe lopez will have a tenth day in the malaya rosa a 12-second lead over carabas and almeida but in fourth place now jai hindley with roman baldy having gone home a big stage coming next the cheetah gets real it's up and down all day on the outskirts of churin for the shortest of the road stages at this gino d'italia almost like a mini liege pastor or a cheerleader perhaps just 147 kilometers not much flat at all and a really dangerous stage there'll be a huge battle to be in the break but behind the gc men will be wary remember you can watch it on gcm plus discovery plus and on the television on eurosport you
